Memory Pager · Calm 라운드

Quiet Signal에서 뻗어나온, 조용한 10가지

앞선 라운드가 강한 개성을 겨뤘다면, 이번엔 반대로 절제입니다. 선택하신 Quiet Signal을 북극성 삼아, 바탕·타입·레이아웃·단 하나의 조용한 시그니처만 달리한 calm·미니멀 형제 열 가지. 강한 색·무거운 텍스처·장식은 걷어내고 여백과 정밀함으로 구분됩니다.

10 calm directions3 hero screens each 30 real Flutter rendersanchor · Quiet Signal (07)

추천 요약 · Where to start

For a 2-person team, three stand out. 12 Foolscap is the best all-rounder: warmest and most on-theme for a memory album, highly legible, cheap to build, and its late-setting drop-cap is a distinctive signature that still reads quiet. 16 Low Lamp is the mood and differentiation pick — the only dark sibling, purpose-built for an end-of-day ritual and instantly recognizable; choose it if evening intimacy outweighs daytime use, and budget time to fix the amber/dark contrast for accessibility. 15 Dateline is the restraint-and-simplicity champion: nothing to animate, no color system, trivial to build and maintain, yet the ink-only 'our daily edition' framing feels deliberate rather than plain. If the team prizes poetry over build cost, 19 Vellum is the most evocative (layered memory made literal) but is the fiddliest and carries a genuine ghost-under-text legibility risk worth a spike before committing. Treat 11 Cold Press, 17 Folio, and 18 Pale Index as safe-but-interchangeable grayscale baselines — pick one only if you explicitly want the plainest look — and note 14 Quadrille's breathing grid is the weakest on accessibility.

12 Foolscap · 따뜻함·최고 올라운더16 Low Lamp · 무드·유일한 다크15 Dateline · 절제·최소 빌드
화면
11

Cold Press

Gallery Mat ☀ light 빌드 Low

Cool-stone gallery: one hero memory matted in a wide off-white passe-partout.

✦ A single 0.5px inset keyline framing the lone memory; zero motion.

  • Simplest possible build — one static item, one hairline, no animation or color logic
  • Maximum legibility: high-contrast Slate ink on Mist, generous whitespace, nothing competing
  • Timeless, safe gallery calm that will not look dated
  • Risks reading as generic 'default minimal' — the least distinctive sibling
  • 0.5px hairline can drop out on low-DPI screens; single-item focus wastes space for browsing

적합 Teams wanting the safest, most legible minimal baseline and a savor-one-memory ritual.

12

Foolscap추천

Printed Book ☀ light 빌드 Low

Memories read as a printed book: one entry per spread, wide literary margins, warmest ground.

✦ An old-style drop-cap that sets a half-beat late, fading in like ink settling.

  • Warmest and most emotionally fitting for a memory album — reads as a written keepsake
  • Delayed drop-cap is a charming, memorable signature with a near-trivial animation
  • Excellent reading legibility from generous leading and a measure-capped column
  • One-entry-per-page slows browsing a large archive
  • Needs a good old-style serif stack; the drop-cap can tip into 'precious'

적합 Couples who want each memory to feel like a page in a shared novel.

13

Manila

Paper Folder ☀ light 빌드 Low

An oat manila folder: memories on dry index-card panels, the shared pet on a tabbed card.

✦ One rounded folder tab, ink-outline only (never filled), marking the active section.

  • Warm, papery, MUJI-tidy; the tab gives clear, functional section orientation
  • Very simple build — static panels, one outlined tab, no motion
  • Legible Bister-on-Manila with a friendly filed-keepsakes metaphor
  • Office/folder metaphor can feel administrative rather than tender
  • Ochre-tan accent is so low-presence it barely reads

적합 Organizers who like a tidy archive with clear sections (great for the shared-pet tab).

14

Quadrille

Graph Paper ☀ light 빌드 Low

Engineer's plotting paper: doodles and the pet sit plotted on a coordinate — calm through precision.

✦ A faint 5mm graph lattice that fades in only under active content and breathes at low contrast.

  • Most technical/precise sibling — a distinctive graph-paper signature
  • Cool, orderly calm that suits doodle-heavy, plotted content
  • Clean Graphite ink stays legible over the pale lattice
  • A breathing low-contrast grid adds visual noise and is an accessibility risk for low-vision users
  • Snapping content to grid intersections is the most layout-constrained build here, and the technical vibe fights tender content

적합 Design- or engineering-minded couples who find graph-paper precision soothing.

15

Dateline추천

Ink Gazette ☀ light 빌드 Low

A two-person gazette: a running masthead over one lead-story memory, pure ink-only two-tone.

✦ A hairline column rule plus a small dateline stamp ( · 화요일 · 07.10 · ); no chroma at all.

  • Simplest to build and maintain — no color system, no animation, just a rule and a stamp
  • Maximum restraint reads as deliberate and confident; excellent two-tone legibility
  • The daily 'edition' framing is a lovely shared ritual and the dateline stamp is a specific, memorable touch
  • Ink-only can feel austere or severe without any warm accent
  • Masthead-plus-single-lead limits information density

적합 Restraint purists wanting a daily 'our edition' ritual with the least to build.

16

Low Lamp추천

Dark / Dim ☾ dark 빌드 Medium

The one dark sibling: a single memory lit like a dim desk lamp on warm near-black, made for 11pm.

✦ A soft lamp-halo that breathes once — a slow 6-8s swell of warm light, then settles.

  • Strongest mood and instant differentiation — the only dark, evening-ritual sibling
  • Genuinely low-stimulus and easy on the eyes for nighttime check-ins
  • Warm-dark intimacy fits a wind-down, together-at-night use better than any light sibling
  • Dark-only with no light mode limits daytime use
  • Low-opacity amber on a dim ground risks failing WCAG contrast; the breathing glow/halation can bother some eyes

적합 An intimate end-of-day ritual app; the strongest pick when mood matters most.

17

Folio

Bound Folio ☀ light 빌드 Low

The album as a bound book on neutral fog: a running header and numbered leaves you turn in order.

✦ A corner folio counter incrementing like page turns (014 / 128); tabular-mono, no motion or color.

  • Simple, buildable — running header, hairline rules, a trivial mono counter
  • Very legible near-black on fog, and the header keeps you oriented
  • The X/128 counter quietly conveys collection scale and a satisfying sense of progress
  • Strict numbered order limits free browsing
  • Can read as a document/manual rather than something warm; overlaps the other grayscale siblings

적합 Teams wanting a finite, orderly, paged archive with a sense of moving through the whole book.

18

Pale Index

Grayscale Index ☀ light 빌드 Low

Calm-tech grayscale: memories filed down one time-spine like library index cards, one lit at a time.

✦ A hairline underline that draws itself left-to-right under a memory on open — a quiet 'read' tick.

  • Information-first vertical timeline is fast to scan and easy to build
  • The self-drawing underline is a tasteful, single, purposeful affordance
  • High-contrast Graphite-on-paper legibility
  • Grayscale clinical-calm is the least warm — can feel emotionally flat for memories
  • Visually overlaps Cold Press and Folio, so its distinctiveness is thin

적합 The notes-app faction who want a clinical, information-first timeline.

19

Vellum

Vellum Overlay ☀ light 빌드 Medium

Sheets of tracing vellum over warm white: today's doodle ghosts faintly through yesterday's — depth without shadow.

✦ One ~85%-opacity vellum panel slides over the ground with prior memory ghosting beneath; one layer at a time.

  • Most poetic, memory-evoking metaphor — layered recollection made literal
  • Airy warm-white translucency is the most distinctive-within-restraint of the light siblings
  • The single sliding panel is an elegant, gentle motion cue
  • Ghosting beneath text is a real legibility/contrast risk that needs careful opacity tuning
  • The fiddliest light sibling to build and QA (layer management, transitions)

적합 Couples who love the poetry of layered memory, and a team willing to tune the translucency.

20

Segment

Porcelain Panel ☀ light 빌드 Medium

Each screen a framed unit on a porcelain panel — a labeled strip and content read like a quiet hardware readout.

✦ A single amber status LED that arms and pulses once when a memory arrives; tabular counters tick over.

  • Strongest 'product/instrument' character among light siblings and the closest idiom to the Quiet Signal blink
  • The one-shot amber pulse plus segment counters is a memorable, restrained signature
  • Warmest white ground stays crisp and legible with framed, orderly fields
  • The device/hardware metaphor can feel cool and detached for intimate memories
  • Panel chrome (seams, strip, counters, one-shot pulse) is a bit more to build than the static siblings

적합 Couples who like a calm instrument/device-face aesthetic and want the truest heir to the blink.

실제로 만져보기

열 가지 모두 실행되는 Flutter 앱입니다(이전 10종도 갤러리에 그대로 남아 있습니다). 프로젝트 루트에서 flutter run -d chrome 를 실행하고 좌측에서 #11~#20을 고르면 됩니다. 특정 화면만: ?d=12&s=petHome&solo=1. 마음에 드는 하나를 정하시면 그 방향으로 나머지 화면과 실제 데이터 연동까지 발전시킵니다.
